title: Favor literal syntax for creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments
description: Using literal syntax for creating empty collections (e.g., sets, lists, tuples) and dictionaries with keyword arguments is clearer, easier to read, and performs better.
message: Favor literal syntax for creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments.
recommendation: Use literal syntax when creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments.

noncompliant.description: Creating an empty set using a function call
Creating an empty dictionary using a dictionary literal
Creating an empty dictionary using a function call
Creating an empty dictionary with keyword arguments using a function call

compliant.description: Creating an empty set using literal syntax (set())
Creating an empty dictionary using literal syntax ({})
Creating an empty dictionary with keyword arguments using literal syntax ({})

why_is_this_an_issue: Using literal syntax for creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments is clearer, easier to read, and performs better.
how_to_fix: Replace function calls with the corresponding literal syntax when creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments.

metadata.title: Favor literal syntax for creating empty collections or dictionaries with keyword arguments
